steps.title: Identify the contrast pattern
steps.content: The passage states that curtailments are named more often than efficiencies. The example given—19.6% of respondents saying “turn off the lights”—is a curtailment.

steps.title: Find an efficiency action at a lower percentage
steps.content: Choice D provides efficient light bulbs at 3.6%, an efficiency action that creates the downward contrast needed after the 19.6% curtailment example. Choices A and B incorrectly pair actions with percentages from the table, and C presents another curtailment instead of an efficiency. That matches choice D — D is correct.
